strongest_supported_claim: Corrected independent proposals match tested HMC observables with shorter critical-region autocorrelation.
main_boundary: One 2D phi4 study; support tails and total-cost comparison remain open.

strongest_supported_claim: Path objectives can lower weight variance for unnormalized targets.
main_boundary: Discretization, training and transfer to lattice physics vary by method.

strongest_supported_claim: Learned independent proposals can be corrected and made symmetry aware.
main_boundary: Training, support and realistic-QCD scaling remain unresolved.

strongest_supported_claim: Learned layers improve tested nonequilibrium estimators while retaining correction.
main_boundary: Protocol length and updated degrees of freedom can dominate cost.

strongest_supported_claim: Symmetry, forces and cross-volume training improve tested sample quality.
main_boundary: Raw samples can remain biased and score/correction cost is substantial.

strongest_supported_claim: Sequential models plus MH reproduce tested scalar/spin observables.
main_boundary: Small systems; no realistic gauge/fermion scaling.

strongest_supported_claim: Explicit scale separation improves the reported near-critical scalar benchmark.
main_boundary: One 2D scalar setting; fine-level ESS still declines.

strongest_supported_claim: Mode-resolved diagnostics and controlled theory reveal failures hidden by aggregate metrics.
main_boundary: They diagnose or explain failure but are not complete samplers.
